name: Robertson v. United States ex rel. Watson
question: Is an action for criminal contempt in a congressionally created court brought in the name and pursuant to the power of a private person, rather than in the name and pursuant to the power of the United States, constitutional?
description: A case, which the Court dismissed as improvidently granted, concerning the constitutionality of actions of criminal contempt brought in the name and pursuant to the power of a private person, rather than in the name and pursuant to the power of the United States.
